---
title: "Summary of tfrmt Table Construction in R"
author:
  - name: "Fuxi Ma"
    email: fuxi.ma@outlook.com
date: "2026-04-12"
format:
  pdf:
    keep-tex: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 5
    number-sections: true
    documentclass: article
    papersize: a4
    geometry: "left=2cm,right=2cm,top=2cm,bottom=2cm"
    linestretch: 1.25
    fontsize: 11pt
    urlcolor: blue
    linkcolor: red
    include-before-body: 
      \thispagestyle{empty}
      \tableofcontents
      \clearpage
    header-includes:
      - \usepackage{titling}
      - \pretitle{\begin{center}\vspace*{2cm}\Huge}
      - \posttitle{\end{center}\clearpage}
      - \raggedbottom 
      - \usepackage{titling}
      - \pretitle{\begin{center}\vspace*{2cm}\Huge}
      - \posttitle{\end{center}\clearpage}
      - \raggedbottom
      - \widowpenalty=10000
      - \clubpenalty=10000
      - \brokenpenalty=10000
editor: visual
execute:
  cache: true
---

{{< pagebreak >}}


## Overview

The following R code demonstrates how to build a publication‑ready table using the **`tfrmt`** package. It starts with a manually created dataset, progressively adds formatting, ordering, and styling, and finally shows how to reuse a template.

## Step‑by‑Step Summary

### 1. Data Preparation
- A `tibble` called `data` is created with columns `Group`, `Label`, `Column`, `Param`, and `Value`.
- It contains summary statistics (n, mean, SD, percentages) for two groups ("Age (y)" and "Sex") across three columns ("Placebo", "Treatment", "Total").
- Percentages are multiplied by 100 because `tfrmt` rounds but does not automatically format as percentages.
- Two ordering columns (`ord1`, `ord2`) are added to control row order.

### 2. Basic `tfrmt` Definition
- A minimal `tfrmt` object specifies `group`, `label`, `column`, `param`, and `value`.
- `print_to_gt()` renders the table as a `gt` object.

### 3. Adding a `body_plan` (Cell Formatting)
- A `body_plan` uses `frmt_structure` rules:
  - Default rule: combine `n` and `pct` as `"{n} ({pct}%)"`.
  - Separate rule for rows with `Label = "n"` to display only `n` as `"XX"`.
  - Separate rule for `Group = "Age (y)"` and `Label = "Mean (SD)"` to combine `mean` and `sd` as `"{mean} ({sd})"`.

### 4. Adding a `col_plan` and Sorting
- Layered arguments:
  - `sorting_cols = c("ord1", "ord2")` to order rows.
  - `col_plan = col_plan(-starts_with("ord"))` to drop helper columns.

### 5. Adding a `row_grp_plan` (Row Group Spacing)
- A `row_grp_plan` adds blank lines after each row group with `post_space`.

### 6. Complete Final `tfrmt`
- All components are combined into a single `tfrmt` call that produces the final table.

### 7. Using a Template (`tfrmt_n_pct()`)
- The code shows how to start from a predefined template and layer custom overrides to avoid repetition.

## Complete R Code

```r
library(tibble)
library(tfrmt)
library(dplyr)

# Create dataset
data <- tibble(Group = rep(c("Age (y)", "Sex", "Age (y)", "Sex"), c(3, 3, 6,12)),
               Label = rep(c("n", "Mean (SD)", "Male","Female"), c(6, 6,6,6)),
               Column = rep(c("Placebo", "Treatment", "Total"), times = 8),
               Param = rep(c("n", "mean", "sd", "n", "pct", "n", "pct"),  c(6, 3, 3, 3,3,3,3)),
               Value = c(15,13,28,14,13,27,73.56, 74.231,71.84,9.347,7.234,8.293,8,7,15,8/14,7/13,15/27,6,6,12,6/14,6/13,12/27)) %>% 
  mutate(Value = case_when(Param == "pct" ~ Value * 100, TRUE ~ Value), 
         ord1 = if_else(Group == "Age (y)", 1, 2),
         ord2 = if_else(Label == "n", 1, 2))

# Basic tfrmt
first_tfrmt <- tfrmt(
  group = Group, label = Label, column = Column, param = Param, value = Value
)
first_tfrmt %>% print_to_gt(data)

# Add body_plan
tfrmt(
  group = Group, label = Label, column = Column, param = Param, value = Value,
  body_plan = body_plan(
    frmt_structure(group_val = ".default", label_val = ".default", 
                   frmt_combine("{n} ({pct}%)", n = frmt("XX"), pct = frmt("x.x")))
  )
) %>% print_to_gt(data)

# More complete body_plan
rounded_tfrmt <- tfrmt(
  group = Group, label = Label, column = Column, param = Param, value = Value,
  body_plan = body_plan(
    frmt_structure(group_val = ".default", label_val = ".default", 
                   frmt_combine("{n} ({pct}%)", n = frmt("XX"), pct = frmt("xx.x"))),
    frmt_structure(group_val = ".default", label_val = "n", frmt("XX")),
    frmt_structure(group_val = "Age (y)", label_val = "Mean (SD)",
                   frmt_combine("{mean} ({sd})", mean = frmt("xx.x"), sd = frmt("x.xx")))
  )
)
rounded_tfrmt %>% print_to_gt(data)

# Add sorting and col_plan
ordered_tfrmt <- rounded_tfrmt %>% 
  tfrmt(sorting_cols = c(ord1, ord2), col_plan = col_plan(-starts_with("ord")))
ordered_tfrmt %>% print_to_gt(data)

# Add row_grp_plan
added_space <- ordered_tfrmt %>% 
  tfrmt(row_grp_plan = row_grp_plan(row_grp_structure(group_val = ".default", element_block(post_space = "  "))))
added_space %>% print_to_gt(data)

# Final complete tfrmt
tfrmt(
  group = Group, label = Label, column = Column, value = Value, param = Param, 
  sorting_cols = c(ord1, ord2),
  body_plan = body_plan(
    frmt_structure(group_val = ".default", label_val = ".default",
                   frmt_combine("{n} ({pct})", n = frmt("X"), pct = frmt("xx.x"))),
    frmt_structure(group_val = ".default", label_val = "n", frmt("XX")),
    frmt_structure(group_val = "Age (y)", label_val = "Mean (SD)", 
                   frmt_combine("{mean} ({sd})", mean = frmt("XX.X"), sd = frmt("x.xx")))
  ),
  col_plan = col_plan(-starts_with("ord")),
  row_grp_plan = row_grp_plan(row_grp_structure(group_val = ".default", element_block(post_space = " ")))
) %>% print_to_gt(data)

# Using a template
tfrmt_n_pct() %>%
  tfrmt(group = Group, label = Label, column = Column, value = Value, param = Param, 
        sorting_cols = c(ord1, ord2),
        body_plan = body_plan(
          frmt_structure(group_val = ".default", label_val = "n", frmt("X")),
          frmt_structure(group_val = "Age (y)", label_val = "Mean (SD)", 
                         frmt_combine("{mean} ({sd})", mean = frmt("XX.X"), sd = frmt("x.xx")))
        ),
        col_plan = col_plan(-starts_with("ord")),
        row_grp_plan = row_grp_plan(row_grp_structure(group_val = ".default", element_block(post_space = " ")))
  ) %>%
  print_to_gt(data)

# Complete R Code

```{r}
#| page-break: false

library(tibble)
library(tfrmt)
library(dplyr)

# Here we will make our first table using tfrmt 

# Create the dataset that matches the slides
data <- tibble(Group = rep(c("Age (y)", "Sex", "Age (y)", "Sex"), c(3, 3, 6,12)),
               Label = rep(c("n", "Mean (SD)", "Male","Female"), c(6, 6,6,6)),
               Column = rep(c("Placebo", "Treatment", "Total"), times = 8),
               Param = rep(c("n", "mean", "sd", "n", "pct", "n", "pct"),  c(6, 3, 3, 3,3,3,3)),
               Value = c(15,13,28,14,13,27,73.56, 74.231,71.84,9.347,7.234,8.293,8,7,15,8/14,7/13,15/27,6,6,12,6/14,6/13,12/27
               )
) %>% 
  # Note because tfrmt only does rounding we will need to have the percents multiplied by 100 
  mutate(Value = case_when(Param == "pct" ~ Value * 100,
                           TRUE ~ Value), 
         ord1 = if_else(Group == "Age (y)", 1, 2),
         ord2 = if_else(Label == "n", 1, 2)) 
data


# set columns in tfrmt ----------------------------------------------------

first_tfrmt <- tfrmt(
  group = Group,
  label = Label, 
  column = Column, 
  param = Param, 
  value = Value
)

# now print this out ------------------------------------------------------
first_tfrmt %>% 
  print_to_gt(data)



# add body_plan ------------------------------------------------------------

tfrmt(
  group = Group,
  label = Label, 
  column = Column, 
  param = Param, 
  value = Value,
  body_plan = body_plan(
    frmt_structure(group_val = ".default", label_val = ".default", 
                   frmt_combine("{n} ({pct}%)",
                                n = frmt("XX"),
                                pct = frmt("x.x"))
    )
  )
) %>% 
  print_to_gt(data)

# Notice that some of the ns don't have percents with them so those show up as
# missing. Also, the means and sds don't have any formatting applied to them.
# So, lets add some more formatting.

rounded_tfrmt <- tfrmt(
  group = Group,
  label = Label, 
  column = Column, 
  param = Param, 
  value = Value,
  body_plan = body_plan(
    frmt_structure(group_val = ".default", label_val = ".default", 
                   frmt_combine("{n} ({pct}%)",
                                n = frmt("XX"),
                                pct = frmt("xx.x"))),
    frmt_structure(group_val = ".default", label_val = "n",
                   frmt("XX")),
    frmt_structure(group_val = "Age (y)", label_val = "Mean (SD)",
                   frmt_combine("{mean} ({sd})",
                                mean = frmt("xx.x"),
                                sd = frmt("x.xx")))
  )
) 

rounded_tfrmt %>% 
  print_to_gt(data)



# add col_plan ------------------------------------------------------------

# It looks better, but now the rows are out of order and we would like to get
# rid of those order columns. By this point, we have done lots of typing. I
# don't really want to write all that again. So we are going to layer.

# this orders the rows 
rounded_tfrmt %>% 
  tfrmt(
    sorting_cols = c(ord1, ord2),
  ) %>% 
  print_to_gt(data)

# add in the col plan 
ordered_tfrmt <- rounded_tfrmt %>% 
  tfrmt(
    sorting_cols = c(ord1, ord2),
    col_plan = col_plan(-starts_with("ord"))
  ) 

ordered_tfrmt %>% 
  print_to_gt(data)

# add row_grp_plan -------------------------------------------------------


# Looking even better!! The last thing we will do is add some formatting of the
# row groups and their labels.

# Indent nested row labels and add a space after groups of rows
added_space <- ordered_tfrmt %>% 
  tfrmt(row_grp_plan = 
          row_grp_plan(
            row_grp_structure(group_val = ".default", element_block(post_space = "  "))
          )
  ) 

added_space %>% 
  print_to_gt(data)


# Here is the full tfrmt  -------------------------------------------------

tfrmt(
  group = Group,
  label = Label,
  column = Column,
  value = Value,
  param = Param, 
  sorting_cols = c(ord1, ord2),
  body_plan = body_plan(
    frmt_structure(group_val = ".default", 
                   label_val = ".default",
                   frmt_combine("{n} ({pct})",
                                n = frmt("X"),
                                pct = frmt("xx.x")
                   )
    ),
    frmt_structure(group_val = ".default", label_val = "n", frmt("XX")),
    frmt_structure(group_val = "Age (y)", label_val = "Mean (SD)", 
                   frmt_combine("{mean} ({sd})", 
                                mean = frmt("XX.X"),
                                sd = frmt("x.xx")
                   )
    )
  ),
  col_plan = col_plan(-starts_with("ord")),
  row_grp_plan = row_grp_plan(
    row_grp_structure(group_val = ".default", element_block(post_space = " "))
  )
) %>% 
  print_to_gt(data)


# templates ---------------------------------------------------------------

# One of the great things about layering is that you can make templates to be
# reused for lots of tables. 
tfrmt_n_pct() %>%
  tfrmt(group = Group,
        label = Label,
        column = Column,
        value = Value,
        param = Param, 
        sorting_cols = c(ord1, ord2),
        body_plan = body_plan(
          frmt_structure(group_val = ".default", label_val = "n", frmt("X")),
          frmt_structure(group_val = "Age (y)", label_val = "Mean (SD)", 
                         frmt_combine("{mean} ({sd})",   
                                      mean = frmt("XX.X"),
                                      sd = frmt("x.xx")
                         )
          )
        ),
        col_plan = col_plan(-starts_with("ord")),
        row_grp_plan = row_grp_plan(
          row_grp_structure(group_val = ".default", element_block(post_space = " "))
        )
  ) %>%
  print_to_gt(data)

```
